question_text: Which of the following correctly describes what happens to an astronaut's weight when they travel from Earth to the Moon?

options.label: A
options.text: Their weight decreases because the Moon has a weaker gravitational field than Earth.

options.label: B
options.text: Their weight stays the same because mass never changes.

options.label: C
options.text: Their weight increases because they are further from the centre of the Earth.

options.label: D
options.text: Their weight disappears because there is no gravity on the Moon.

explanation: Weight depends on gravitational field strength (W = m × g). The Moon's gravitational field is much weaker than Earth's (about 1.6 N/kg compared to 10 N/kg), so an astronaut's weight would be much less. Their mass stays constant, but weight changes.
